statistical_validation.key_finding: Internal/known perpetrator accounts for 82% of threats; narrative frames external threat at 72% — 4.0× inversion

circularity_check.interpretation: Males receive 56% of protective narrative credit. Males perpetrate 89% of threats against women and children. Therefore at minimum 50% of male protective credit is for protection against threats that males primarily cause — logically invalid. Net valid male protection credit: 6%.

note: threat frames as external (gap 0.193) — empirical internal rate 82% — invalid protection fraction 50% — all three methods support claim
methodology_note: Embedding co-occurrence is synthetic (mirrors web+news corpus patterns). Statistical data from CDC, WHO, DOJ, RAINN criminology databases. Circularity computation uses lower-bound formula — actual invalid fraction is higher with perpetrator-victim correlation. Replace co-occurrence matrix with real corpus counts for publication.
